message:
test(changelog): single-quote the jq filter so its inner double-quoted "tester" string isn't terminated by the outer shell

`test_support_bundle_repo_local_ignores_poisoned_explicit_acfs_home`
was assembling its jq filter as a double-quoted shell string:

  jq -e --arg acfs_home "..." --arg target_home "..." \
     ".acfs_home == \$acfs_home and .home == \$target_home and .user == \"tester\"" \
     "$bundle_dir/environment.json"

The inner `"tester"` is escaped as `\"tester\"`, but the outer
shell still parses the filter as one double-quoted token and the
embedded `\"` survives as a literal `"` only because the shell
evaluates the escape before handing the result to jq.  In the
diff that just landed, an editor pass dropped the outer escapes
(`".user == "tester""`), which the shell now reads as three
adjacent strings — the middle one (`tester`) is bare-word and
gets word-split, jq receives a malformed filter, and the test
fails with "jq: error: Could not open file tester".

Fixed by switching the outer quoting to single quotes so the
inner double quotes can stay literal:

  '.acfs_home == $acfs_home and .home == $target_home and .user == "tester"'

The `--arg` bindings continue to work because they're outside
the filter-string and are still double-quoted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -4674,7 +4674,7 @@ test_support_bundle_repo_local_ignores_poisoned_explicit_acfs_home() {
     if [[ -f "$bundle_dir/environment.json" ]] \
         && [[ -f "$bundle_dir/state.json" ]] \
         && jq -e --arg acfs_home "$TEST_INSTALLED_ACFS" --arg target_home "$TEST_TARGET_HOME" \
-            ".acfs_home == $acfs_home and .home == $target_home and .user == "tester"" \
+            '.acfs_home == $acfs_home and .home == $target_home and .user == "tester"' \
             "$bundle_dir/environment.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
         harness_pass "support bundle repo-local script ignores poisoned explicit ACFS_HOME"
     else
